Activity: Refined the project problem statement — Rewrote the problem description to better capture the technical needs and measurable outcomes.
Met with the client to clarify requirements — Asked targeted engineering questions to fill in missing specifications or constraints.
Reason: Understanding client needs to determine constraints

Activity: Collected stakeholder feedback — Gathered input from users, technicians, or advisors to understand real-world operational needs.
Reason: Empathizing with stakeholders to inform the design

Activity: Created initial engineering requirements — Drafted early performance requirements such as loads, voltages, flow rates, or tolerances.
Updated project scope based on new information — Added or removed features after learning more about constraints or user needs.
Reason: Improving plan based on new information

Activity: Developed early system goals — Outlined what the final engineered system should achieve functionally and technically.
Set up team roles and responsibilities — Defined each member’s primary engineering responsibilities (CAD, circuits, software, testing).
Created the initial Gantt chart — Built a timeline showing design, prototyping, testing, and review milestones.
Identified project constraints — Documented limits such as cost caps, weight restrictions, power limits, or safety rules.
Reviewed existing documentation from the client — Analyzed drawings, manuals, or reports to understand current system performance.
Conducted literature review on similar engineering systems — Summarized previous academic or industry approaches.
Researched relevant industry standards — Identified applicable ASME, IEEE, ISO, ASTM, or NFPA guidelines.
Compared existing commercial solutions — Looked at specs, features, and performance claims of similar products.
Reason: Exploring existing products to inform the design

Activity: Looked up patents related to our design — Reviewed patent diagrams and claims to understand prior art.
Analyzed benchmark product specifications — Compared power, size, capacity, efficiency, and reliability across products.
Reason: Unclear whether the team is prototyping their own design and doing analysis for an evaluation, or gathering information (understand space)/synthesizing information to plan their design

Activity: Investigated material options — Researched suitable metals, polymers, composites, or electronic components.
Reason: Exploring materials to use in design

Activity: Studied similar engineering projects from past years — Identified successful design choices and common pitfalls.
Evaluated cost ranges of comparable systems — Gathered typical pricing for main components and subsystems.
Reason: Synthesizing cost range information for design

Activity: Reviewed safety regulations applicable to our design — Determined whether OSHA, UL, IEC, or other rules apply.
Researched environmental impact considerations — Looked at recyclability, emissions, power consumption, and waste.
Sketched multiple design concepts — Produced quick mechanical or electrical sketches to visualize alternatives.
Brainstormed alternative engineering solutions — Generated as many feasible approaches as possible.
Developed a functional block diagram — Broke the system into major functions and linked them logically.
Reason: My take on this is that they are documenting their current prototype to better understand its performance. However, this borders on needing more evidence to tell how far along they really are

Activity: Created use-case scenarios — Outlined how the product will be used in real operational environments.
Reason: I think this falls under implementation, as it documents best use for the final product

Activity: Built a morphological chart for concept combinations — Explored all valid combinations of subsystem options.
Reason: Creating a list of all possible combinations leads to new ideas. It's thinking about the possibilities.

Activity: Generated rough CAD sketches — Created early 3D shapes to evaluate layout ideas and geometry.
Proposed preliminary subsystem layouts — Organized sensors, motors, circuits, or components on a rough system layout.
Discussed feasibility with faculty advisors — Presented high-level concepts and received expert critique.
Reason: My take on this is that they're using the feedback to narrow their design focus

Activity: Identified potential risks in early concepts — Listed failure points, difficult integrations, or unreliable assumptions.
Reason: This seems to be part of the planning phase- identifying weaknesses before moving forward

Activity: Narrowed concepts to a top set for evaluation — Selected the most promising alternatives for deeper analysis.
Reason: Narrowing concepts to determine evaluation plan

Activity: Built a concept selection matrix — Compared the top alternatives using weighted criteria.
Reason: Already have alternatives and now providing a method to select which one is most viable.

Activity: Evaluated manufacturability of each concept — Assessed how easy each concept is to machine, print, or assemble.
Reason: The team seems to be preparing to prototype components

Activity: Analyzed cost estimates for major alternatives — Calculated approximate subsystem and total system costs.
Reason: The team is considering alternatives to the solution, which is part of the evaluation process of prototyping

Activity: Assessed engineering feasibility of proposed concepts — Checked whether the design can meet required performance levels.
Reason: This is likely ideation, as students are determing whether the design is viable for moving forward

Activity: Compared performance trade-offs — Evaluated speed vs. cost, size vs. strength, accuracy vs. complexity.
Reason: I think the team is using information that they gathered to determine the performance metrics they need to prioritze

Activity: Met with the client to present concept options — Received approval or feedback on engineering direction.
Gathered advisor feedback on technical direction — Incorporated faculty suggestions into updated concepts.
Reason: Using feedback to iterate on design

Activity: Updated concept diagrams — Improved or corrected earlier sketches and diagrams based on feedback.
Selected final design direction — Chose the concept the team will carry forward to detailed design.
Reason: Narrowed plan for finalized design direction

Activity: Documented rationale for concept selection — Wrote an explanation supporting the final choice using data.
Reason: This document justifies final design decisions

Activity: Created a detailed system architecture diagram — Mapped all subsystems and their interactions.
Reason: This could be ideating, where they're planning out the design system to prototype. It could also be synthesizing, where they're using their preliminary info to develop a potential architecture diagram that will be refined during brainstorming. Or, they may be evaluating their prototype. We need to know how deep they are in their process

Activity: Defined subsystem boundaries — Specified what each subsystem is responsible for and what it is not.
Reason: This could be planning or synthesizing- more evidence would help determine where the team is in the design process

Activity: Developed the interface specification between subsystems — Documented mechanical mounts, electrical connections, and communication protocols.
Produced a preliminary bill of materials (BOM) — Built a list of parts including estimated costs and sources.
Estimated overall system cost — Aggregated subsystem costs into a full project-level estimate.
Reason: Planning the cost of developing their full design

Activity: Updated the system block diagram — Refined signal flows and function groupings.
Identified required sensors/actuators — Selected candidate components based on required ranges and accuracy.
Outlined control system requirements — Defined feedback loops, sampling rates, and control logic.
Calculated high-level performance estimates — Estimated forces, power consumption, speeds, or output levels.
Reason: Same reasoning as for the fluid flow calculation task

Activity: Conducted preliminary FMEA — Listed potential failure modes and assessed severity and likelihood.
Reason: The FMEA should be the precursor to building and testing a prototype

Activity: Developed detailed CAD models — Produced full 3D part models with realistic geometry.
Ran preliminary stress calculations — Evaluated loads using hand calculations or simple FEA.
Designed preliminary circuits — Drafted schematics showing power paths, sensors, and signal processing.
Developed a wiring diagram — Created a map of all electrical connections in the system.
Reason: Same as the previous task with the block diagram

Activity: Created a rough PCB layout — Designed a board outline and arranged components.
Built MATLAB/Simulink models — Simulated system dynamics or control loops.
Reason: The team seems to be building and testing software for their design.

Activity: Designed initial control algorithms — Wrote pseudocode or flowcharts for system behavior.
Modeled thermal loads — Estimated heat generation and necessary cooling approaches.
Calculated fluid flow or pressure requirements — Applied basic fluid mechanics to design piping or pumps.
Reason: This could fit a couple different spaces but in my opinion the best fit is ideate. The team is determining the requirements that will guide their design. I'm going to assume they're doing this using information they gathered earlier in the process

Activity: Selected materials and components based on calculations — Justified choices using engineering properties.
3D printed prototype parts — Produced low-cost physical models to verify geometry.
Reason: Creating prototypes fits the prototype space. Verifying geometry is an evaluation of the parts

Activity: Machined or fabricated simple components — Created metal or plastic parts using shop tools.
Soldered prototype circuits — Built early boards for testing signals and connections.
Assembled a breadboard circuit — Prototyped electronic functionality quickly.
Reason: Developed a prototype to test a concept

Activity: Set up test rigs for experiments — Built fixtures or mounts to support repeatable testing.
Reason: Setting up rigs to evaluate components

Activity: Built first mechanical prototype — Created an early version of structural or moving parts.
Developed prototype firmware — Wrote initial code for reading sensors or driving actuators.
Created early software UI — Implemented basic screens, control panels, or visualization tools.
Manufactured a second iteration of prototype parts — Corrected earlier geometry or fit problems.
Performed basic fit-checks on assembled modules — Verified that parts interface correctly.
Wrote a test plan — Documented what tests will be done, how, and why.
Built a test procedure for a subsystem — Created step-by-step instructions so results are repeatable.
Set up instrumentation for data collection — Configured sensors, DAQs, oscilloscopes, or logging software.
Performed load or stress tests — Measured structural or mechanical capacity under load.
Measured electrical performance — Checked voltages, currents, noise levels, or signal integrity.
Ran motion tests on mechanical components — Observed actuation speed, friction, or alignment.
Conducted usability testing with sample users — Asked representative users to try the prototype.
Reason: Even though users are involved, this is still in the prototyping phase as the testing will lead to iteration

Activity: Recorded test data for analysis — Stored measurements in spreadsheets or databases.
Identified failure modes from test results — Found weak points or unexpected behaviors.
Updated design based on test findings — Made revisions to improve performance or reliability.
Integrated mechanical components — Assembled moving parts, frames, mounts, or enclosures.
Connected sensors to the microcontroller — Set up wiring, connectors, and pin assignments.
Loaded and tested firmware on the prototype — Uploaded code and checked sensor readings or actuator outputs.
Merged hardware and software subsystems — Connected physical components to code running on the controller or PC.
Debugged integration issues — Fixed miswiring, incorrect assumptions, or code errors.
Verified electrical connections and signal integrity — Checked continuity, noise, and power stability.
Calibrated sensors and actuators — Performed procedures to ensure correct readings or motions.
Reason: The team seems to be working with design components

Activity: Updated wiring or routing after integration problems — Rerouted cables or changed component placement.
Conducted full-system dry run — Operated the system without load or under safe conditions.
Documented integration progress — Wrote down findings, problems, and next steps.
Reason: I think this sort of documentation supports future replication

Activity: Updated weekly engineering notebook entries — Documented work completed, results, and challenges.
Revised design documentation — Updated drawings, schematics, or models after changes.
Prepared slides for weekly meetings — Created visuals showing data, models, and progress.
Wrote a progress report for the team — Summarized tasks completed and planned work.
Met with advisors for design review — Presented updates and received technical feedback.
Reason: Team is using feedback to improve design

Activity: Submitted updated CAD or code to repository — Uploaded revisions to GitHub, OneDrive, or a shared drive.
Updated risk register — Added new risks or revised likelihood/severity ratings.
Collected and summarized teammate tasks — Compiled individual contributions into a team-level report.
Reason: The team is debriefing their work so far. If they were delivering to stakeholders, this might be considered implementation. However, we don't have evidence that that has happened

Activity: Prepared material for Preliminary Design Review (PDR) — Organized system-level design documentation and slides.
Prepared material for Critical Design Review (CDR) — Created detailed design evidence including calculations, drawings, and test data.
Reason: This process evaluates the current design so that it can be improved (not the final design presentation, which could be considered implementation)

Activity: Optimized CAD models for reduced material usage — Reduced wall thicknesses and added ribs or fillets while confirming safety factors with quick FEA checks.
Reviewed tolerance analysis for key components — Updated tolerance stack-ups and revisited fits to ensure assembly remains robust under worst-case conditions.
Performed circuit simulations to validate design assumptions — Used SPICE or equivalent tools to check gain, bandwidth, and stability before ordering boards.
Updated control system block diagrams — Refined sensor, actuator, and feedback connections to reflect the current architecture.
Adjusted actuator selection based on torque analysis — Compared datasheets and recalculated stall/continuous torque margins for expected duty cycles.
Reason: The team is making adjustments to an existing design, which fits prototyping and iterating

Activity: Revised thermal model after updated heat-load assumptions — Recomputed junction temperatures and heatsink requirements using new power estimates.
Conducted supplier research for long-lead components — Identified alternatives and documented lead times and minimum order quantities to reduce schedule risk.
Reason: The team seems to know the components they will need for the design, which means they're planning out how to source these effectively

Activity: Completed wiring harness revisions — Shortened runs, added strain relief, and updated labels to improve serviceability and safety.
Updated PCB routing to reduce interference — Separated analog and digital grounds, tightened return paths, and improved decoupling placement.
Held internal design review with team — Walked through open issues, captured action items, and recorded decisions in the design log.
Reason: Making and documenting decisions regarding process

Activity: Produced detailed engineering drawings — Added dimensions, tolerances, GD&T, and notes sufficient for fabrication without ambiguity.
Reason: Using drawings to represent ideas and plan for prototyping

Activity: Created assembly instructions for prototype build — Authored step-by-step procedures with photos and torque specs for repeatable assembly.
Reason: These procedures are likely a deliverable for others to replicate the design

Activity: Validated sensor accuracy using calibration equipment — Compared readings against a reference instrument and computed calibration coefficients.
Bench-tested microcontroller firmware — Verified peripheral drivers, interrupt timing, and error handling on a development board.
Researched IP ratings for enclosure design — Determined sealing, gasket, and venting needs to achieve the target ingress protection level.
Generated CFD mesh for airflow modeling — Built a clean geometry and verified mesh quality metrics before running simulations.
Refined FEA boundary conditions — Adjusted constraints and loads to better reflect real mounting and contact conditions.
Prepared parts list for machine shop — Consolidated material specs, quantities, and drawing numbers to streamline fabrication.
Ordered electronic components for prototype — Placed BOM orders, tracked shipment ETAs, and documented substitutions when needed.
Laser-cut prototype panel components — Converted CAD to cut files and validated hole sizes and kerf compensation after fabrication.
Reviewed machining tolerances with shop staff — Discussed critical dimensions and finish requirements to prevent rework.
Implemented version control for simulation files — Organized models and results with clear commit messages and tagged baselines.
Reason: Organizing results to improve the design

Activity: Conducted cost-benefit analysis for different materials — Compared performance, machinability, and cost to select the most economical option that meets requirements.
Reason: The team seems to be using the CBA to plan the best material to use for their design

Activity: Performed weldment design calculations — Checked joint design, throat sizes, and residual stress considerations against expected loads.
Designed custom test fixture components — Modeled fixtures to constrain parts and enable repeatable measurements during testing.
Reason: Creating parts to test other parts

Activity: Analyzed system noise sources — Traced EMI/RFI and mechanical vibration pathways and noted mitigation strategies.
Reason: This is most likely a testing phase, which fits into prototyping

Activity: Evaluated subsystem redundancy options — Considered backup sensors or failover modes to improve reliability for critical functions.
Developed calibration routine for embedded system — Scripted a guided procedure that stores offsets and scale factors in nonvolatile memory.
Reason: This is a process for users to implement with the final product

Activity: Reviewed industry standards for compliance — Mapped requirements to design features and created a gap list for remediation.
Reason: Using information to determine compliance needs

Activity: Printed engineering drawings for advisor review — Prepared redline-ready sets and captured feedback for drawing updates.
Updated BOM with supplier lead times — Flagged long-lead items and proposed order dates to protect the build schedule.
Measured deflection of prototype under load — Applied known forces and compared measured displacement to analytical predictions.
Performed time-study on assembly process — Timed each step to identify bottlenecks and opportunities for mistake-proofing.
Completed hazard analysis for prototype testing — Documented pinch points, hot surfaces, and electrical hazards with mitigations.
Added safety interlocks to design — Incorporated switches and software checks that prevent unsafe operation states.
Reason: This seems to best fit prototyping because they are adding components to their design. There's not enough evidence to suggest evaluation (which should follow this task), which would mean that they are either creating or iterating. Without evidence of iteration, I selected create as the best fit. *However* this task is borderlining not having enough evidence to evaluate

Activity: Integrated wireless communication module — Brought up drivers, paired devices, and verified data throughput and latency.
Validated motor driver operation — Checked current limits, thermal behavior, and fault reporting under various loads.
Rewrote firmware for better timing performance — Optimized ISR priorities and reduced blocking calls to improve responsiveness.
Developed GUI improvements based on user feedback — Simplified workflows, added status indicators, and improved error messages.
Validated algorithm behavior using test datasets — Ran recorded data through the code and compared outputs to expected results.
Updated design notebook with new test results — Inserted plots, tables, and interpretation notes tied to requirement IDs.
Revised power budget calculations — Accounted for worst-case current draw, startup surges, and standby consumption.
Conducted failure-mode experiments on subsystem — Intentionally induced faults to confirm protections and recovery behavior.
Reason: Using testing to push design to failure

Activity: Created vibration test plan — Defined excitation profiles, instrumentation, and pass/fail criteria for resonance checks.
Ran endurance test on mechanical subsystem — Executed extended duty cycles to evaluate wear and thermal stability.
Analyzed test data in MATLAB — Cleaned datasets, computed statistics, and generated plots to summarize performance.
Reason: This may be prototyping or ideating- more evidence is needed to determine what the group hoped to accomplished with the test data/ where they are in their design process

Activity: Created troubleshooting guide for team members — Documented common failure symptoms, root causes, and quick checks.
Reason: Similar to other documentation deliverables, I think this is something that can be implemented for longterm use and replication

Activity: Discussed results in weekly advisor meeting — Presented highlights, open issues, and proposed next steps for approval.
Reason: Proposing next steps in design process

Activity: Produced updated layout for enclosure — Adjusted component placement to improve airflow, service access, and cable routing.
Designed mounting bracket for internal components — Sized fasteners and checked stiffness to minimize vibration and misalignment.
Conducted compliance checklist review — Stepped through applicable clauses and recorded objective evidence for each item.
Reviewed client feedback and made design changes — Tagged change requests and implemented the highest-impact updates first.
Measured current draw of prototype system — Captured steady-state and transient currents to validate the power supply margin.
Validated communication between subsystem modules — Exercised message traffic and confirmed error handling across interfaces.
Tested error-handling routines in firmware — Injected simulated faults and confirmed graceful recovery without data loss.
Replaced faulty prototype components — Diagnosed suspect parts, swapped them, and retested the affected functions.
Reason: Iterating on design using improved components

Activity: Performed alignment checks on rotating assemblies — Used dial indicators and feeler gauges to minimize runout and misalignment.
Measured thermal response using infrared camera — Identified hotspots and verified that component temperatures stayed within limits.
Updated integration diagram after testing revisions — Reflected rerouted cables and swapped parts in the latest system schematic.
Prepared data summaries for project documentation — Condensed large datasets into key metrics, tables, and representative plots.
Drafted poster for design exposition — Outlined the problem, approach, results, and impact with clear visuals.
Reason: As this is likely a final deliverable, I think it's fair to say that they're implementing their product

Activity: Recorded demonstration videos for testing — Captured setup, procedure, and outcomes to share with stakeholders asynchronously.
Reason: Capturing performance for stakeholders to evaluate

Activity: Fabricated updated prototype components — Implemented design changes and checked compatibility with existing assemblies.
Created custom cabling for internal connections — Cut to length, crimped terminals, and applied labels for traceability.
Revised safety documentation for lab testing — Updated SOPs, PPE requirements, and emergency procedures based on new risks.
Completed subsystem acceptance tests — Ran defined checks and documented pass/fail results against requirements.
Ran benchmark tests on prototype performance — Compared the system against baseline targets and noted deltas.
Installed prototype in testing environment — Mounted the unit, connected services, and verified readiness for trials.
Measured pressure drops in fluid pathways — Instrumented the system and confirmed losses matched the model within tolerance.
Conducted leak tests on enclosures or lines — Applied pressure or vacuum and monitored for decay and soap-bubble indications.
Balanced rotating components — Applied trial weights and used a balancer to reduce vibration to acceptable levels.
Cleaned and organized lab bench for efficiency — Labeled bins, routed cables, and set ESD protection for safer work.
Reason: In my opinion, this borders on a task that doesn't count as part of the design process. However, because they're preparing to work in the lab, I think it fits the organize process

Activity: Built a small-scale mock-up to test ergonomics — Checked reach, visibility, and access for maintenance tasks.
Reason: Used a model to test performance

Activity: Validated sensor linearity across operating range — Collected calibration points and computed regression error metrics.
Carried out code review with teammates — Examined logic, naming, and edge cases; documented findings in the repo.
Reason: This seems to be a synthesis process although further evidence could clarify that they're actually prototyping

Activity: Implemented logging and telemetry in firmware — Added timestamped events and ring buffers to aid future debugging.
Refactored software modules for clarity — Split complex functions, added unit tests, and improved documentation.
Created unit tests for control algorithms — Tested boundary conditions and failure modes using simulated inputs.
Assessed manufacturability with DFM checklist — Reviewed features prone to warpage, tool access, and unnecessary complexity.
Reason: Evaluating the design's features for implementation

Activity: Updated 3D print settings for strength and accuracy — Tuned infill, layer height, and orientation; validated dimensions post-print.
Negotiated quotes with vendors — Requested revisions on pricing and lead time, and documented final terms.
Qualified alternate suppliers for risk mitigation — Compared quality history and requested samples to diversify sourcing.
Created acceptance criteria for incoming parts — Defined inspection points, tolerances, and sampling plans for receiving.
Built a requirements traceability update — Linked new tests and design changes back to specific requirement IDs.
Estimated reliability using simple models — Applied parts-count or MIL-HDBK methods to approximate mean time between failures.
Checked fastener selection and torque specs — Verified grade, thread engagement, and locking features suit the application.
Reason: They seem to be planning the selections for their design (as opposed to testing the design they're building)

Activity: Added identification labels and markings — Developed label layouts for serial numbers, safety icons, and wiring IDs.
Reason: There is not enough evidence here to determine which space the team may have been in. Labeling could be organizing (i.e., synthesis), or it could be part of the ideation or prototyping process

Activity: Reviewed enclosure venting and filters — Sized vents and selected filter media to balance cooling and contamination control.
Reason: selecting components for design based on requirements

Activity: Documented open questions for the client — Compiled a focused list to unblock decisions on features and priorities.
Reason: My take is that these questions can be used to identify design decisions and next steps, which likely means that the team is synthesizing current information and identifying what they still need

Activity: Reworked prototype after discovering interference — Modified features and reprinted or machined parts to clear collisions.
Stood up a continuous integration pipeline for code — Automated builds and lint checks to prevent regressions.
Prepared a maintenance checklist for the prototype — Listed periodic inspections, lubrication points, and firmware updates.
Reason: I consider this to be a deliverable to help users maintain the design over time

Activity: Conducted a design-to-cost review — Identified high-cost drivers and proposed design simplifications or part swaps.
Updated risk register with mitigation status — Closed resolved items and added triggers for new emerging risks.
Built a simplified digital twin for testing — Created a lightweight model to evaluate control logic before hardware changes.
Ran a pilot test with an external user — Observed real usage, captured feedback, and logged issues for triage.
Prepared shipping and handling guidelines — Specified packaging, shock indicators, and handling precautions for transport.
Reason: I'm interpreting this as preparation for the final product

Activity: Compiled a bill of process for assembly — Outlined tooling, sequence, and takt time for a consistent build.
Reason: The bill of process helps others replicate the team's work

Activity: Created a spare parts list and lifecycle notes — Identified wear items and listed vendor part numbers with expected lifetimes.
Reason: Planning wear items and how to source replacements

Activity: Finalized lessons-learned notes for the team — Captured what worked, what didn’t, and recommendations for future cohorts.
Reason: Making recommendations for sustaining the design
